title: Bao Buns
summary: Here's the insider info on how to make the fluffiest, softest bao buns in town. Make them solo or bring in the crew and make an afternoon of it.

method.heading: Whisk The Yeast.
method.description: Tip the yeast into a bowl and add 240ml warm water. Whisk well and set aside for 10 mins.

method.heading: Make The Dough.
method.description: Tip the flour, baking powder and sugar into a large mixing bowl. Slowly add water mix and then oil. Add salt and mix together, then tip onto a clean surface and knead for 10 mins.

method.heading: Rest The Dough.
method.description: Add dough to a bowl and add a small layer of oil to top of dough – I like to use sesame oil but any oil will do. Cover with cling film.

method.heading: Proof.
method.description: Leave to prove for 40-60 mins or until the dough has doubled in size, then remove the dough on a well-floured table top and lightly knead till the dough is smooth

method.heading: Shape Your Bao.
method.description: Get some scales and weigh out chunks of dough into 55g balls, shaping them into smooth balls (the smoother the better).

method.heading: Proof The Again.
method.description: Place them on a tabletop or baking sheet a couple inches apart, cover lightly with cling film and let them prove for another 20-30 mins, until puffed up.

method.heading: Roll Out Your Bao.
method.description: Take each ball and with a rolling pin roll them into an oval shape, trying to get them about 1cm thick, then brush lightly with oil, I use sesame.

method.heading: Fold The Bao.
method.description: Carefully fold in half and place inside your steamers. Repeat this process until they’re all done, put the steamer lid on and let them sit for another 30-40 mins.

method.heading: Steam.
method.description: Get a pan big enough to sit the steamers on and bring the temperature up to a rolling boil. Once the water has come up to temp, add the steamers on top and let them steam for 10-12 minutes until the buns are fluffy.
